intended_use: The REMI-AI Discrete Detection Module (REMI-AI DDM) is indicated for the analysis of REMI Remote EEG Monitoring System electroencephalogram (EEG) recordings. REMI-AI DDM is intended to be used by phys
intended_use_full: The REMI-AI Discrete Detection Module (REMI-AI DDM) is indicated for the analysis of REMI Remote EEG Monitoring System electroencephalogram (EEG) recordings. REMI-AI DDM is intended to be used by physicians qualified to analyze and interpret EEG who will exercise professional judgment in using the information. As an aide to the qualified physician's REMI EEG review, REMI-AI DDM marks previously acquired sections of REMI EEG that may correspond to neurological events of interest indicative of potential electrographic seizures lasting at least 10 seconds in duration. REMI-AI DDM is indicated for use with adult and pediatric patients (6+ years). REMI-AI DDM does not mark REMI EEG records in real time and does not provide any diagnostic conclusion about the patient's condition to the user.
ground_truth: Consensus determination by panel of 3 independent expert epileptologists (6 available) with certification by American Board of Psychiatry and Neurology or American Board of Clinical Neurophysiology with Special Competency in Epilepsy Monitoring, based on review of standard 19+ channel wired 10-20 montage EEG records; consensus required agreement from at least 2 of 3 members for presence or absence of electrographic seizure event
other_metrics: Event-level Sensitivity 86.2% (95% CI lower bound 79.5%); Mean Per-Patient Sensitivity 92.2% (95% CI Lower Bound 86.5%, range 50-100%); False Alarm Rate (FAR) 0.162 FP/hr (415 FP across 2,562.5 hours of data, 95% CI upper bound 0.221 FP/hr); Mean Per-Patient FAR 0.176 FP/hr (95% CI Upper Bound 0.230, range 0-0.929 FP/hr)
